intro: Campomanesia adamantium, also known as Guanábana de Monte or Guava-fig tree, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family (Myrtaceae) native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential anti-diabetic and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Campomanesia adamantium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being used in topical applications such as ointments and creams.
